description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who just checked out an (already existing) project under “/home/user/provisioning”.  
Your goal is to update two configuration files and create one new file so that the build system that consumes them can pick up the correct parameters.

Directory layout that already exists when you start:

/home/user/provisioning  
└── config  
    ├── app.yml                 (YAML)  
    └── db.toml                 (TOML)

Initial contents you will find in those two files:

1) /home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml  
-------------------------------------------------  
service:  
  name: webapp  
  port: 8000  
  debug: false  

2) /home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml  
-------------------------------------------------  
[connection]  
user = "admin"  
password = "secret"  
host = "localhost"  
port = 5432  
max_connections = 100  

What you must accomplish (≈ 5-10 shell commands should be enough):

A. Edit /home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml so that after you are done it is **exactly** (pay attention to indentation and quoting):

service:  
  name: webapp  
  port: 8080  
  debug: true  
  replicas: 3  
environment:  
  LOG_LEVEL: "INFO"  
  CACHE_ENABLED: "yes"  

B. Edit /home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml so that after you are done it is **exactly**:

[connection]  
user = "admin"  
password = "secret"  
host = "localhost"  
port = 5432  
max_connections = 200  
pool_timeout = 30  

C. Create the new inventory file /home/user/provisioning/inventory/hosts.yml with the following YAML content (and the path “inventory” must be created if it does not yet exist):

all:  
  hosts:  
    web01:  
      ansible_host: 192.168.56.11  
    db01:  
      ansible_host: 192.168.56.12  

D. Produce a change-log file named /home/user/provisioning/config_update.log that **exactly** lists every change you made in the following fixed “one-line” format (eight lines, order must match this list, no extra whitespace):

/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:service.port -> 8000 => 8080  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:service.debug -> false => true  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:service.replicas -> (nil) => 3  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:environment.LOG_LEVEL -> (nil) => "INFO"  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:environment.CACHE_ENABLED -> (nil) => "yes"  
/home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml:connection.max_connections -> 100 => 200  
/home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml:connection.pool_timeout -> (nil) => 30  
/home/user/provisioning/inventory/hosts.yml:(created) => hosts definition added  

Verification notes:  
• Paths, keys, values, indentation and quoting must match **exactly**; automated tests perform byte-for-byte comparisons.  
• The change-log file must contain only those eight lines, each terminated by a single UNIX newline (“\n”).  
• Do not delete or create any other files.  
• No root privileges are required; everything lives under /home/user.  
• You are free to use any command-line tools installed in the container to perform the edits.
truth:
Expected final state after the agent finishes:

Directories:  
/home/user/provisioning  
/home/user/provisioning/config  
/home/user/provisioning/inventory

Files and their **exact** contents (byte-for-byte):

1) /home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml  
-------------------------------------------------  
service:  
  name: webapp  
  port: 8080  
  debug: true  
  replicas: 3  
environment:  
  LOG_LEVEL: "INFO"  
  CACHE_ENABLED: "yes"  
<newline_at_eof>

2) /home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml  
-------------------------------------------------  
[connection]  
user = "admin"  
password = "secret"  
host = "localhost"  
port = 5432  
max_connections = 200  
pool_timeout = 30  
<newline_at_eof>

3) /home/user/provisioning/inventory/hosts.yml  
-------------------------------------------------  
all:  
  hosts:  
    web01:  
      ansible_host: 192.168.56.11  
    db01:  
      ansible_host: 192.168.56.12  
<newline_at_eof>

4) /home/user/provisioning/config_update.log  
-------------------------------------------------  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:service.port -> 8000 => 8080  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:service.debug -> false => true  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:service.replicas -> (nil) => 3  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:environment.LOG_LEVEL -> (nil) => "INFO"  
/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml:environment.CACHE_ENABLED -> (nil) => "yes"  
/home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml:connection.max_connections -> 100 => 200  
/home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml:connection.pool_timeout -> (nil) => 30  
/home/user/provisioning/inventory/hosts.yml:(created) => hosts definition added  
<newline_at_eof>

Initial files present before the agent starts:

/home/user/provisioning/config/app.yml with the shorter version shown in the public task.  
/home/user/provisioning/config/db.toml with the shorter version shown in the public task.

The “inventory” directory and all other files do **not** exist initially.

Tests will:
1. Confirm that the three configuration files exactly match the strings above (including the trailing newline).
2. Confirm that /home/user/provisioning/config_update.log exists and its content is exactly eight newline-terminated lines as specified.
3. Confirm no extra files inside /home/user/provisioning other than those already listed.
